title: The Cautious Amorist
description: The Cautious Amorist is a novel by Norman Lindsay. Banned in Australia from 1933 to 1953.
descriptionParagraphs: The Cautious Amorist is a novel by Norman Lindsay. Banned in Australia from 1933 to 1953.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a novel, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Australia. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned in Australia from 1933 to 1953. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: The Cautious Amorist is a novel by Norman Lindsay. Banned in Australia from 1933 to 1953.
banningParagraphs: The Cautious Amorist entered censorship debates as a novel associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1933 in Australia, where Australia authorities banned publication or circulation. Banned in Australia from 1933 to 1953. Banned in Australia from 1933 to 1953.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.

counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
